LAW AND MOTION 302, NO APPEARANCE ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING.RESPONDENT WILLIAM DOUGLASS' MOTION FOR SANCTIONS (NO STIPULATION TO JPT AT 8/13/12 HEARING). THE COURT ADOPTS, IN PART, THE RECOMMENDATION OF PRO TEM JUDGE HALPERIN AS FOLLOWS: CONTINUED TO AUGUST 31, 2012 AT 9:30 AM IN DEPARTMENT 302. RESPONDENT SHALL FILE AND SERVE A SUPPLEMENTAL BRIEF AND SUPPORTING EVIDENCE THAT SHALL BE ORGANIZED IN A WAY FOR THE COURT TO SEGREGATE BY CATEGORY THE AMOUNTS FROM THE TOTAL CLAIMED, INCLUDING ITEMIZATIONS FOR: (1) OPPOSING NATIONWIDE'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT; AND (2) CREATING RESPONDENT'S ANALYSIS OF MILEAGE DRIVEN BY THE DECEASED BEFORE NATIONWIDE PRODUCED THE DISPATCHER'S LOGS. SUCH SUPPLEMENT BRIEF SHALL BE FILED NO LATER THAN AUGUST 27, 2012 AT 4 PM. PETITIONER MAY FILE AND SERVE A RESPONSIVE BRIEF NO LATER THAN AUGUST 28, 2012 AT 4 PM. BOTH BRIEFS SHALL BE LIMITED TO NO MORE THAN 5 PAGES. SERVICE SHALL BE EFFECTUATED BY EMAIL, FAX OR PERSONAL DELIVERY. COURTESY COPIES SHALL BE PROVIDED TO DEPARTMENT 302 ON THE DATE OF FILING. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER, CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; COURT REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER CSR # 5976 =(302/MJM)

Respondent William Douglass' Motion For Sanctions (No Stipulation To Jpt At 8/13/12 Hearing)
Off Calendar Aug-27-2012 Continued To Aug-31-12 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
Respondent William Douglass' Motion For Sanctions (No Stipulation To JPT At 8/13/12 Hearing).
The court adopts, in part, the recommendation of Pro Tem Judge Halperin as follows: Continued to August 31, 2012 at 9:30 am in Department 302. Respondent shall file and serve a supplemental brief and supporting evidence that shall be organized in a way for the court to segregate by category the amounts from the total claimed, including itemizations for: (1) opposing Nationwide's motion for summary judgment; and (2) creating Respondent's analysis of mileage driven by the deceased before Nationwide produced the dispatcher's logs. Such supplement brief shall be filed no later than August 27, 2012 at 4 pm. Petitioner may file and serve a responsive brief no later than August 28, 2012 at 4 pm. Both briefs shall be limited to no more than 5 pages. Service shall be effectuated by email, fax or personal delivery. Courtesy copies shall be provided to Department 302 on the date of filing. Judge: Marla J. Miller, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Court Reporter: Sherry Sawyer CSR # 5976 =(302/MJM)
